summary: Good ambient recording despite occasional wind, highlighted by a memorable Louie Louie opening the second set.
review: The EC‑7 cardioid mic capture sounds fairly clear, with only a little wind noise that doesn’t detract much from listening. Musically the show shines thanks to an unexpected Louie Louie kickoff of the second set, which reviewers singled out as a highlight. Overall it’s an enjoyable snapshot of the Frost Amphitheatre shows from late April/early May 1988.

ai_rating.rationale: Single 4‑star review praises both the performance (notably Louie Louie) and the recording quality, indicating a good show with an adequately captured audio source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: EC‑7 cardioid mics pick up stage sound well; some wind hiss is audible at times but overall the recording remains clear and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set opens strongly with Louie Louie, creating an energetic momentum for the rest of the performance.
